title: Generalize decider's integrity intake (Step 1d) from moderate-only to every-severity, and trigger off integrity's already-emitted tracked_under:null signal instead of a severity-specific branch — model-class-resilient, dedup-respecting, urgent-fast-path.
depends_on: ec95134 (2026-05-06) — the original integrity-moderate handoff fix; PROP-037 generalizes that fix to every severity
structure-integrity.md (line 565) — integrity already emits tracked_under on every finding regardless of severity; PROP-037 makes decider respect that signal as the trigger
headline_recommendation: Replace the severity-specific branch in decider-intake.md Step 1d (currently moderate-only) with a single severity-agnostic loop that iterates EVERY entry in integrity_report.issues_found and creates an ISS for any finding where tracked_under is null/missing. This is Option D (hybrid path-pin + explicit loop), refined: the trigger is integrity's already-emitted tracked_under field, not severity. Side-effects: (a) every severity gets ISS-routed identically; (b) integrity's existing dedup work is respected (no duplicate dedup in the decider); (c) the model-class-resilient phrasing uses rigid IF/THEN with explicit JSON field extraction, so Sonnet and Opus produce the same output. Operator reviews before any prompt edit lands. Self-apply NONE of this — propose-only.

executive_summary.problem_one_line: Integrity's 2026-05-15T01:16Z report flagged 5 findings (1 critical + 2 major + 1 moderate + 1 minor), all with tracked_under:null; decider's 01:30Z run (14 min later, fresh access) created ZERO ISSs from any of them, forcing the operator to hand-apply all 5 fixes at 05:00-05:45Z this morning. Same pattern across at least the prior 8 decider runs (2026-05-13 → 2026-05-16): 0 integrity-derived ISSs across the entire window, despite each integrity report carrying tracked_under:null findings.
executive_summary.root_cause_one_line: decider-intake.md Step 1d's explicit ISS-creation procedure is scoped only to `severity: "moderate"` findings. Critical/major/minor findings fall back to vague pre-task#91 guidance ('treat critical as priority 1', 'flag for immediate rebuild') with NO concrete ISS-creation mechanism, so even when decider reads the integrity report, no ISS is created. The fix is structural, not a model-class compatibility issue — both Sonnet and Opus runs missed these findings identically because the prompt itself omits the relevant procedure for non-moderate severities.
executive_summary.fix_one_line: Replace the moderate-specific branch with a single severity-agnostic loop iterating issues_found[]; trigger on tracked_under==null; create one ISS per untracked finding regardless of severity, with severity propagated from the finding into the ISS; respect integrity's already-emitted tracked_under signal as the dedup oracle (don't re-dedup in the decider — integrity already did that work). Surface results in daily report's recommended_actions as one concise line per integrity report.
executive_summary.scope_clarification: Design only. Operator reviews PROP-037 before any prompt edit lands. The change touches one reference file (monitor/prompts/reference/decider-intake.md) in two places: (a) generalize the existing moderate-specific block to severity-agnostic; (b) keep the introductory 'overall_status: fail → treat as priority 1' shorthand but reframe it as 'in addition to ISS creation, treat critical findings as same-run priority'. NO scheduled-task change. NO integrity-side change required — integrity is already emitting the right signal.

directive_proposed_three_hypotheses: A — Code regression: Step 1d was wired up by task #91 in early May 2026; something refactored the intake flow.
B — Model-class compatibility: Sonnet may be skipping integrity intake due to literal path/scanning differences vs Opus.
C — Filter too narrow: Step 1d only handles moderate severity; critical+major+minor slip through.

git_log_intake_md_since_2026_04_01: ec95134 (2026-05-06) — original integrity-moderate handoff gap fix; landed Step 1d's moderate-specific block
b13d162 (2026-05-06) — PROP-022 phase 2 human-notes archive split (touches Step 1a, not Step 1d)
bdc733b (2026-05-06) — PROP-022 phase 3 priority-queue.json archive split (touches Step 1h2, not Step 1d)
9db26e3 (2026-05-09) — Rename morning-briefing → latest-decider-summary (touches summary references, not Step 1d)
a9fbd67 (2026-05-09) — PROP-025 curmudgeon batch-gate (touches Step 1f new-WIN onboarding, not Step 1d)
38d40ed (2026-05-11) — PROP-028 stale-assessment skip (touches Step 1h2 predictions, not Step 1d)
4ed9ccb (2026-05-15) — Workspace sync (sync commit, no semantic change)
evidence_collected.git_log_conclusion: Step 1d's integrity-intake block has NOT been touched since ec95134 landed it on 2026-05-06. Hypothesis A (regression) is RULED OUT.

decider_run_audit.note: ISS-2014 created (DOI verification, unrelated to integrity); the 2026-05-15T01-16 integrity report's 5 findings (1 critical + 2 major + 1 moderate + 1 minor) all ignored

Mentioned 'Integrity report 2026-05-15T01:16 flagged build_drift and workspace_files_at_risk — likely stale' in recommended_actions but did NOT create an ISS. Proves decider IS reading the integrity report — the gap is in the action procedure, not the read.

evidence_collected.run_audit_conclusion: 8 consecutive decider runs across 2026-05-13 → 2026-05-16, ZERO integrity-derived ISSs. The 17:20 run proves decider IS reading the integrity report at least sometimes (it cited build_drift and workspace_files_at_risk in recommended_actions). What's missing is the action: when decider reads a tracked_under:null finding, it does NOT create an ISS because the prompt only prescribes that action for severity:moderate. Even Opus runs in the window (most of these were Opus per scheduler) missed the findings identically. Hypothesis B (model-class) is therefore NOT the primary cause — both Sonnet and Opus follow the prompt literally; the prompt itself is the gap. Hypothesis B may compound the problem on Sonnet (more literal adherence) but isn't the root.

step_1d_audit.verbatim_filter_quote: **Moderate findings (`severity: "moderate"`)** — must be triaged on EVERY decider run.
step_1d_audit.scope_of_iss_creation_in_current_prompt: Only `severity: "moderate"` findings → explicit ISS-creation block with JSON template and conventions.
step_1d_audit.scope_of_other_severities: `overall_status: "fail"` (i.e., contains at least one critical) → 'treat critical issues as priority 1' (vague, no ISS template). Major/minor have NO procedure at all.
step_1d_audit.integrity_side_signal_already_exists: structure-integrity.md line 565 (introduced same commit ec95134): 'For every finding (any severity), before emitting it, search ... If matched, set tracked_under: "ISS-NNN" on the finding. Decider's Step 1d ... uses this signal: a finding with tracked_under set is no-op'd by the decider (already-tracked); a finding with tracked_under: null triggers ISS creation.' Integrity emits the signal for every severity — but decider's Step 1d uses severity as the trigger, not tracked_under. Asymmetric wiring.

evidence_collected.step_1d_audit_conclusion: Hypothesis C (filter too narrow) is CONFIRMED. The prompt is severity-gated where it should be tracked_under-gated. Integrity already does the right thing (emits tracked_under on every finding); decider's contract is wrong (only acts on the moderate slice). The original task #91 commit message frames the fix as 'integrity-moderate handoff gap' — scope was bounded to moderates by name. The structural fix to all severities was never authored.

hypothesis_evaluation.final_diagnosis: Hypothesis C is the root cause; Hypothesis A is ruled out; Hypothesis B is a possible amplifier but not the cause. The fix must generalize Step 1d to every severity AND must be phrased model-class-agnostically (rigid IF/THEN, explicit JSON field extraction, pinned path) so neither Sonnet's literal-mindedness nor a future model-swap creates a new gap.

Q1_option_recommendation.recommended: Option D-refined — hybrid path-pin + explicit severity-agnostic loop, triggered on integrity's already-emitted tracked_under field rather than severity
Q1_option_recommendation.rationale: Option A (path pin alone) doesn't address the severity-filter gap. Option B (explicit severity loop) doesn't address the model-class-resilience concern around field extraction. Option C (integrity emits machine-readable findings.json) is unnecessary — integrity already emits structured JSON; the report-*.json is already machine-readable. Option D-refined combines: pinned path (monitor/integrity/report-*.json, glob to newest), explicit JSON parsing (one specific field set: severity, category, location, description, suggested_fix, tracked_under), tracked_under==null as the trigger (uses integrity's already-done dedup work), rigid IF/THEN phrasing (no judgment cues), and severity-agnostic loop (every untracked finding gets an ISS).
Q1_option_recommendation.model_class_resilience_specifics: The prompt phrasing must explicitly say: (1) 'Glob monitor/integrity/report-*.json, pick newest by filename timestamp.' (2) 'Parse as JSON, iterate r.issues_found[].' (3) 'For each finding where tracked_under == null OR tracked_under is absent: create an ISS — do not skip based on severity.' (4) 'Use exactly these fields: id=ISS-{next_id}, title=Integrity {severity}: {description.slice(0,80)}, severity={finding.severity}, category=integrity_finding, location={finding.location}, suggested_fix={finding.suggested_fix}, source=integrity-{report_filename_date}, created_by=decider-step1d-untracked-finding.' (5) 'After all findings processed, emit one line to recommended_actions: ‹Integrity report YYYY-MM-DDTHH-MM had N findings: X already-tracked, Y created.›' No judgment-cued phrasing ('consider', 'if appropriate', 'as needed'). Every step is mechanical text/JSON manipulation.

Q2_idempotency_across_decider_runs.design: Decider's 2-3 runs/day vs integrity's 1 run/day creates the question of re-processing. The answer: don't track a last-processed-mtime in the decider. Instead, rely on integrity's tracked_under signal — once decider creates ISS-N for a finding, the NEXT integrity run will find that ISS-N in open-issues.json and set tracked_under: "ISS-N" on the same finding. Then when decider re-reads the report on its next run, tracked_under is no longer null, so no duplicate ISS is created. The dedup work happens integrity-side, deterministically, between runs.
Q2_idempotency_across_decider_runs.edge_case: What if decider reads the same integrity report twice before integrity re-runs? E.g., decider 01:30 creates ISS-N from finding F; decider 05:30 re-reads the same 01:16 report, where finding F still has tracked_under:null (integrity hasn't run again to update it). Mitigation: at ISS-creation time, decider also does a defensive open-issues.json lookup on title-match (Integrity {severity}: {description.slice(0,80)}) — if an ISS with that exact title exists and was created in the last 24h, skip. This is the same minimal dedup the current moderate-block already performs, generalized.
Q2_idempotency_across_decider_runs.alternative_rejected: Tracking last_processed_integrity_report_mtime in monitor/decisions/. Adds state file, has its own race conditions, doesn't compose with the existing tracked_under signal. Reject.

Q3_finding_id_for_dedup.design: No new finding_id field required. The composite key (category, location, description.slice(0,80)) is sufficient for cross-run dedup because: (a) integrity already uses category+location for tracked_under matching; (b) the ISS title (Integrity {severity}: {description.slice(0,80)}) becomes the secondary dedup key for the same-day-twice case. Adding an explicit integrity_finding_id would require a structure-integrity.md change to emit it, which expands scope without solving the same-day-twice case any better than title-match does.
Q3_finding_id_for_dedup.future_consideration: If category+location collisions become common (multiple findings with same category+location but distinct descriptions), introduce a finding_id then. Not needed now — 2026-05-15's 5 findings had 5 distinct (category, location) pairs.

Q4_meta_PROP_with_DIRECTIVE_20260514_001.design: Separate PROPs. PROP-036 (analyst summary-write gap) and PROP-037 (this) are both 'invisible-handoff' bugs but the failure mechanisms differ: PROP-036 is missing-write-step (analyst's dispatcher dropped the summary-write postlude for 8/9 modes); PROP-037 is wrong-trigger (decider's intake uses severity instead of tracked_under). Different fix shapes. A meta-PROP would force them into the same template and produce a muddier story.
Q4_meta_PROP_with_DIRECTIVE_20260514_001.meta_pattern_worth_noting: Both are CONTRACT failures, not behavior failures. The agents do exactly what their prompts say; the prompts are wrong. The lesson is: when a handoff is wired up, the read-side and write-side must use the SAME signal (analyst write summary at canonical path ↔ tinker pipeline-health read summary mtime; integrity emit tracked_under ↔ decider trigger on tracked_under). PROP-036 fixed the symmetry on the analyst side; PROP-037 fixes it on the decider side. The meta-pattern is worth a separate longer note (consider for a future PROP on 'handoff-contract symmetry as a pipeline invariant'), but should not block either of these specific fixes.
Q4_meta_PROP_with_DIRECTIVE_20260514_001.no_batching: Per directive's out_of_scope_for_tinker rule, this PROP does not batch with DIRECTIVE-20260514-001 or DIRECTIVE-20260514-002 (curmudgeon split).

counter_argument_pass.operator_preference: Per CLAUDE.md user preference 'Think about the other side of the argument too' and per operator_preferences_for_proposal item: counter-argument section mandatory.

arg_1_integrity_findings_should_be_opera.steelman: The whole point of integrity is to surface things to a human in the daily standup; auto-creating ISSs sends them into the backlog (currently ~230 open items) where they get prioritized along with everything else and lose their dashboard-prominence. The morning summary screen is the right interface for integrity findings, not the issue tracker. ISS-creation might make findings LESS visible, not more.
arg_1_integrity_findings_should_be_opera.rebuttal: Two problems with steelman: (a) the 'morning summary' channel for integrity is already broken — the operator had to manually scrape 5 findings from latest-integrity-summary.txt this morning and hand-apply all 5 because there was no auto-attention mechanism in place. Auto-ISS routes them into a deterministic pipeline that doesn't depend on the operator's morning-attention. (b) The dashboard-prominence concern is real but addressed orthogonally: dashboard prominence comes from severity tier + age (PROP-026 burndown ordering, etc.), not from skipping the ISS step. An integrity-critical ISS with severity=critical and age=0h naturally sorts to the top. The operator-attention argument is sound for ad-hoc inspection but breaks for systematic tracking. ISS creation does not REMOVE the morning-summary visibility — both channels co-exist (decider also emits a recommended_actions line summarizing this run's integrity triage).
arg_1_integrity_findings_should_be_opera.verdict: Auto-ISS is correct. Operator-attention layer stays via latest-integrity-summary.txt + decider's recommended_actions summary line.

arg_2_operator_handled_5_findings_in_45_.steelman: Operator scraped the integrity report and applied all 5 findings between 05:00 and 05:45Z this morning (commits c7b1274 + ecdbcb2). That's 45 minutes of operator labor for a daily cadence. Maybe the right pattern is operator-applies-integrity-in-daily-standups, not auto-route. Add a 'morning integrity batch' to the operator's runbook and call it done. No prompt edit needed.
arg_2_operator_handled_5_findings_in_45_.rebuttal: Two failure modes the steelman doesn't address. (a) URGENT findings can't wait for next-morning operator attention. The 2026-05-15 next_id=384 collision was a MAJOR with same-day risk: every 2h baby run could have allocated a colliding ID until operator-morning, corrupting expansion-tracker.json. 24h operator-turnaround is too slow for URGENT integrity findings. (b) The 'operator handles it' pattern is exactly the labor that the pipeline is supposed to eliminate — every agent handoff that requires operator attention is a missed automation opportunity. The pipeline already has good shape for non-urgent items (ISS routes through normal triage); the only gap is the routing step itself.
arg_2_operator_handled_5_findings_in_45_.verdict: Auto-ISS is correct for SAME-RUN urgency on critical/major; it does no harm for moderate/minor. The operator-as-fallback layer stays via the recommended_actions summary line — operator can scan it to spot anything worth intervening on, but doesn't have to do the routing work.

arg_3_revert_to_opus_not_a_prompt_fix.steelman: Sonnet decider may simply not be ready for intake work this complex. The fix isn't decider-intake.md; it's reverting decider to Opus and accepting the cost-per-run delta. Save the prompt-edit budget for actually-broken-things.
arg_3_revert_to_opus_not_a_prompt_fix.rebuttal: Three reasons the model-class explanation is insufficient. (a) The 2026-05-14T01:27 and 21:29 runs were on Opus (per scheduler config; Sonnet rollout was 2026-05-15) and STILL missed every integrity finding. So the bug exists on Opus too. (b) The 2026-05-15T17:20 Sonnet run DID mention integrity in recommended_actions ('Integrity report ... flagged build_drift and workspace_files_at_risk') but did NOT create an ISS. So the gap isn't 'Sonnet can't find the integrity report' — Sonnet found it. The gap is that the prompt doesn't tell EITHER model to create an ISS for non-moderate findings. (c) Even if Opus were perfectly reliable here, the contract between integrity (emit tracked_under) and decider (act on tracked_under==null) should be model-class-agnostic. Relying on Opus's stronger judgment to paper over a prompt gap is fragile — the next model swap (Haiku optimization, Opus deprecation) re-opens the bug.
arg_3_revert_to_opus_not_a_prompt_fix.verdict: Reverting to Opus is not the right fix. The prompt edit is. (Separate question: should decider be on Sonnet at all? Out of scope for this PROP; raise as separate consideration if cost-per-run analysis warrants it.)

arg_4_severity_proliferation_will_create.steelman: Generalizing to ALL severities will multiply the ISS-creation rate. Today: 1 ISS per integrity run (the moderate). Tomorrow under PROP-037: potentially 5 ISSs per run (critical+major×2+moderate+minor as on 2026-05-15). At 1 integrity run/day, that's 35 ISSs/week purely from integrity, on top of the curmudgeon/poller/analyst feeds. Backlog grows faster.
arg_4_severity_proliferation_will_create.rebuttal: Three responses. (a) The 2026-05-15 5-finding day was an exceptional accumulation precisely because integrity findings have been silently dropping for ≥8 days — the backlog had to surface somewhere, and it surfaced as a single big batch. Under PROP-037, the first few runs will look heavy (clearing accumulated drops), but the steady-state rate falls back to the actual integrity-incident rate, which historical data suggests is 1-2 findings per integrity run on average. (b) Integrity-derived ISSs have a deterministic resolution path — most are 'apply suggested_fix' (workspace artifact cleanup, allocator bump, tracker backfill) that decider can self-apply in the same run, closing the ISS immediately. So the open-bucket impact is low. (c) The dedup-via-tracked_under design (Q2 above) prevents the same finding from creating an ISS twice. Steady-state, ISS creation rate ≈ unique-finding rate, not findings-times-decider-runs.
arg_4_severity_proliferation_will_create.verdict: Backlog-noise concern is real but mitigated by self-apply for mechanical findings + dedup-via-tracked_under for non-mechanical findings. Recommend a follow-up PROP-030 backlog-trend check 30 days post-deploy to confirm the steady-state assumption.

arg_5_overall_status_fail_already_says_p.steelman: Step 1d already says 'overall_status: fail → treat critical issues as priority 1' (line 67). For critical findings, that's the correct guidance — handle them immediately, don't ISS-route them. ISSs are for things that route through the pipeline; critical findings should bypass the pipeline. The current shape is correct for criticals; only the moderate gap needed filling, which task #91 did.
arg_5_overall_status_fail_already_says_p.rebuttal: Three problems. (a) 'Treat as priority 1' has no observable definition. In practice, 'priority 1' means 'someone fixes it now' but there's no contract for what 'someone' or 'fixes it' means. The 2026-05-15T01:30 decider run read the report and DIDN'T treat the build_drift critical as priority 1 — it just ignored it. With no ISS, there's no audit trail of 'decider considered this and decided to defer' vs 'decider missed it entirely.' (b) Same-run-fix and ISS creation are NOT mutually exclusive. Decider can create ISS-N AND apply the fix AND close ISS-N in the same run. The ISS becomes the audit trail. The 2026-05-15T17:20 run shows the failure mode: integrity is mentioned in recommended_actions, no fix applied, no ISS, finding silently re-flagged on the next integrity run. (c) For criticals specifically, the ISS-creation is the URGENT-fast-path the directive asks for. If decider creates ISS-N immediately upon reading the integrity report, and the ISS has severity=critical, the dashboard surfaces it AT THE TOP — that IS 'treat as priority 1' with observable mechanics.
arg_5_overall_status_fail_already_says_p.verdict: The current 'priority 1' shorthand is too soft. ISS creation is what makes 'priority 1' real.

files_NOT_touched: monitor/prompts/structure-integrity.md (integrity-side is already correct — emits tracked_under for every severity)
Any data/*.json or docs/*.html (no content change)

verbatim_replacement_block.location_in_file: decider-intake.md, replace existing block at L64-L103 (the entire '## Step 1d: Integrity Report' section, including the moderate-specific subsection)
new_content_outline: Lead paragraph: explains scope is every-severity, triggered on tracked_under==null
Subsection A: 'Read the latest integrity report'
1. Glob monitor/integrity/report-*.json; pick newest by filename-embedded timestamp.
2. Parse as JSON; let R = parsed.
3. Read R.overall_status and emit one summary line at the top of recommended_actions ('Integrity report TIMESTAMP overall_status=fail/warn/pass').
1. For each finding F in R.issues_found:
a. If F.tracked_under is a string (not null/absent), no action — integrity already deduped this finding against ISS-{F.tracked_under}. Skip.

b. If F.tracked_under is null or absent, run defensive same-day dedup: search open-issues.json for an open ISS where title === ('Integrity ' + F.severity + ': ' + F.description.slice(0,80)) AND created_at within 24h. If found, no action. Else proceed.

c. Create ISS-{next_id} with exact fields: id, title='Integrity {F.severity}: {F.description.slice(0,80)}', description=F.description, location=F.location, category='integrity_finding', severity=F.severity (carry the finding's severity verbatim), status='open', source='integrity-{report_filename_date}', suggested_fix=F.suggested_fix, created_at=now-ISO, created_by='decider-step1d-untracked-finding'. Increment next_id.

d. If F.severity is 'critical' OR 'major' AND F.suggested_fix is structurally applicable (allocator-bump, tracker-backfill, build-run, file-delete) AND decider has self-apply authority for the target file, attempt the fix in the same run; on success, close ISS-{just-created} with closure_reason='same-run-self-apply'. Mechanical fixes only — judgment-required fixes (e.g., 'investigate why X happened') stay open.
Subsection C: 'Surface results in daily report'
1. Emit ONE concise line in recommended_actions: 'Integrity report YYYY-MM-DDTHH-MM had N findings: X already-tracked (skipped), Y created (ISS-A, ISS-B, ...), Z same-run-resolved.'
2. Do NOT echo individual finding descriptions in recommended_actions — they live on the ISSs.

1. Every finding must end in exactly one state: already-tracked (skipped, no-op), created (open ISS), or same-run-resolved (created+closed). 'Wontfix' allowed but must be a created+closed-with-wontfix-reason ISS, never silence.
Anchor reference to existing 'why this matters' paragraph at end (slightly updated wording).

urgent_fast_path_specification.behavior: Same-run ISS creation; same-run self-apply attempt if mechanical; same-run close if self-apply succeeds; recommended_actions summary line emitted regardless.
urgent_fast_path_specification.rationale: Per operator_preferences_for_proposal item #3: 'the next_id=384 collision finding had a same-day collision risk; the design should ensure CRITICAL/MAJOR findings get ISSs created in the same decider run as integrity emits them, not the next run.' This is exactly the contract.

regression_test_post_deploy.manual_check_1: On the run immediately after the prompt edit lands, the next integrity report's findings should each appear as one of: (a) tracked_under-skipped, (b) newly-created ISS, (c) same-run-resolved ISS. Inspect monitor/decisions/daily-report-NEW.json::issues_created for the integrity-finding category.
regression_test_post_deploy.automated_check_1: Add to tinker-pipeline-health.md Mode 1 probe: for each integrity report in last 7 days, check that the corresponding decider run (within +24h) created ISSs equal to the count of tracked_under:null findings in that report. Threshold: any decider run that processed an integrity report with N>0 untracked findings and created 0 integrity_finding-category ISSs → flag as 'integrity-handoff-broken' in tinker findings.
regression_test_post_deploy.rollback_signal: If post-deploy backlog grows >10% week-over-week purely from integrity_finding-category ISSs (per PROP-030 metrics), revisit: tighten the dedup window, or shift mechanical-fix attempts to same-run self-apply to keep ISS count flat.

implementation_path.self_apply_status: NO. Propose-only. Operator reviews PROP-037, approves the design, then either tinker self-applies the prompt edit under PROP-014-amendment-001 Q6 lifecycle OR operator files a separate directive routing the prompt-edit to a specific run.
implementation_path.estimated_diff_size_lines: ~50 lines replacing existing 37-line block (net +13 lines in decider-intake.md). No other file changes.
implementation_path.rollout_safe_to_pause: Yes. The prompt is read on every decider invocation; the change applies immediately on the next decider run after the edit lands. No state-file migration. No data migration.
implementation_path.rollback_path: git revert the prompt-edit commit. Reverts behavior to current moderate-only handling. No cleanup of ISSs created in the interim — they remain valid open issues that route through the normal triage.
PR_ready_checklist: Decider-intake.md edit drafted matching the verbatim_replacement_block outline
test.js gate (no schema change, should pass)
Single-commit landing: 'Apply PROP-037: generalize integrity intake to every-severity via tracked_under signal'

OQ1_severity_minor_threshold.question: Should minor-severity findings (currently 1/5 on 2026-05-15: 'workspace_artifact 2 non-review files in monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/') trigger ISSs? Or threshold at moderate-or-above?
OQ1_severity_minor_threshold.tinker_recommendation: Include minors. The 2026-05-15 minor was a real workspace-hygiene issue that decider could self-apply (delete the two files). Suppressing minors loses that low-cost cleanup loop. If operator disagrees, the threshold parameter can live in CLAUDE.md or in Step 1d itself (one-line edit) rather than the prompt structure.

OQ2_same_run_self_apply_for_critical.question: The urgent-fast-path proposes same-run self-apply for mechanical critical/major fixes. Is operator comfortable with decider auto-applying (e.g., next_id bump, build run) without operator review? Or should ALL critical/major findings just be ISS-created and routed normally?
OQ2_same_run_self_apply_for_critical.tinker_recommendation: Auto-apply only for the 4 categories that are already operator-pre-approved: (a) tracker_allocator next_id bumps (deterministic), (b) workspace_artifact deletion (operator-pre-approved cleanup), (c) build_drift → run 'node build.js publish' (the canonical path), (d) tracker_orphan backfill (mechanical). Everything else (data_mismatch, broken_anchor, broken_link) → ISS-only, route normally. If operator wants ALL → ISS-only with zero same-run self-apply, the URGENT-fast-path softens to 'critical severity sorts to top of next decider run's bucket' rather than 'same-run resolved'. Slight slower but eliminates auto-apply blast radius.

OQ3_should_integrity_emit_finding_id.question: Is operator OK with composite-key dedup (category+location+description.slice(0,80)), or would they prefer integrity emits an explicit finding_id (e.g., 'IF-2026-05-15-build_drift-0') that decider uses?
OQ3_should_integrity_emit_finding_id.tinker_recommendation: Composite key for now. It matches integrity's existing tracked_under dedup logic and avoids a structure-integrity.md change. Revisit only if composite-key collisions become common.

OQ4_decider_model_class_question_out_of_.question: Separate from PROP-037: should decider revert to Opus given evidence of Sonnet shallowness on intake?
OQ4_decider_model_class_question_out_of_.tinker_recommendation: Out of scope for PROP-037. The evidence shows even Opus runs missed these findings (prompt-gap, not model-gap). But the cost-per-run vs intake-quality tradeoff is worth a separate analysis after PROP-037 lands — if PROP-037 closes the gap on both classes, the model question becomes purely cost-driven; if Sonnet still misses findings on the new prompt, the model-class hypothesis warrants its own directive.

verification_pattern.description: How a future tinker run verifies whether PROP-037 has landed and is working
verification_pattern.step_1_prompt_landed: grep -A2 'every-severity' /tmp/edit-clone/monitor/prompts/reference/decider-intake.md → returns >0 lines if the new Step 1d is in place. If 0 lines, prompt edit not landed; PROP-037 is STILL_BROKEN.
verification_pattern.step_2_decider_acting_on_signal: For the most recent decider run after the prompt-edit commit, check daily-report.issues_created for category='integrity_finding' entries OR recommended_actions for a line matching 'Integrity report .* had N findings'. If neither present AND the corresponding integrity report had tracked_under:null findings, prompt edit landed but didn't take — STILL_BROKEN.
verification_pattern.step_3_no_duplicate_iss_per_finding: For the latest integrity report, count tracked_under:null findings = N. Count open ISSs created since the prompt-edit commit with category='integrity_finding' matching those findings (by description.slice(0,80) ≈ ISS title) = M. Expect M ≤ N (each finding → at most one ISS). If M > N, the dedup pattern is broken — STILL_BROKEN.
verification_pattern.step_4_steady_state_backlog_check: After 14 days post-deploy, query PROP-030 queue-history.jsonl for net_velocity_7d trend on category='integrity_finding'. Expect ≤1-2 net ISS/day. If trending positive, follow OQ2 tighter-self-apply discussion.

appendix_evidence_log.step_1d_current_text_quote: **Moderate findings (`severity: "moderate"`)** — must be triaged on EVERY decider run. ... For each `severity: "moderate"` finding in the integrity report: 1. Dedupe against existing tracking. ... 2. If not matched, create an open ISS ... 3. Never silently drop ... 4. Surface in daily report.
appendix_evidence_log.structure_integrity_md_emits_tracked_und: structure-integrity.md L565: 'For every finding (any severity), before emitting it, search monitor/decisions/open-issues.json for an existing ISS that matches this finding by category + location ... If matched, set tracked_under: "ISS-NNN" on the finding. Decider's Step 1d (per reference/decider-intake.md) uses this signal: a finding with tracked_under set is no-op'd by the decider (already-tracked); a finding with tracked_under: null triggers ISS creation.'
appendix_evidence_log.the_asymmetry: Integrity's prompt explicitly states the contract ('Decider's Step 1d ... uses this signal: a finding with tracked_under: null triggers ISS creation'). Decider's prompt does NOT honor that contract — it triggers on severity:moderate instead. The two prompts were written by the same commit (ec95134) but one (integrity) was written to the broader contract while the other (decider) was bounded to the moderate-only scope. PROP-037 aligns the decider side with what integrity already claims is happening.
